name: Human idea two-level B staging follow-through: footprint-neutral B shared permutation on top of the current streaming consumer path

summary.name: Human idea two-level B staging follow-through: footprint-neutral B shared permutation on top of the current streaming consumer path
summary.hypothesis: Round 8 suggests the B-feed family is finally moving the right metrics without raising registers or shared memory, but issue-order alone is not enough. The next step is the user's earlier producer-layout / consumer-layout split, done inside the existing footprint: keep the current single-fragment mirrored consumer path, but change the physical placement of B chunks in shared memory so `stage_b_shared_tile_async` writes a bank-friendlier order and `b_shared_col_from_logical` maps logical consumer columns back onto that permuted layout. This keeps macro tiling, stage depth, and cp.async volume fixed while making the producer and consumer views of B deliberately different.
summary.expected_bottleneck: Warp-local shared/L1 operand delivery and bank behavior on the hot-band B fragment path rather than CTA-level control flow.
summary.risk: Moderate. This is still correctness-sensitive, and the shared permutation has to preserve 16-byte async-copy alignment plus the current footprint. The guardrail is strict: no second B shared tile, no CTA repack, no extra barrier, no stage-depth loss.
summary.stop_condition: Reject if the shared permutation increases footprint, complicates into CTA repack, or leaves hot-band time flat. The family only stays alive while it is shared-neutral and warp-local.

notes: Node C may now implement this one direction.
